feedback.sharing_description: Choose whether voted AI outputs can be shared with Paperclip Labs. This answer becomes the default for future thumbs up and thumbs down votes.

feedback.sharing_explanation_allow: to share this vote and future voted AI outputs. Choose
feedback.sharing_explanation_deny: to keep this vote and future votes local.

devRestart.reason_backend_changes: backend files changed since this server booted

pathInstructions.title: How to get a full path
pathInstructions.description: Paste the absolute path (e.g. /Users/you/project) into the input field.
pathInstructions.mac.step1: Open Finder and navigate to the folder.
pathInstructions.mac.step3: Hold the Option (⌥) key — "Copy" changes to "Copy as Pathname".
pathInstructions.mac.step4: Click "Copy as Pathname", then paste here.
pathInstructions.mac.tip: You can also open Terminal, type cd, drag the folder into the terminal window, and press Enter. Then type pwd to see the full path.
pathInstructions.windows.step1: Open File Explorer and navigate to the folder.
pathInstructions.windows.step2: Click in the address bar at the top — the full path will appear.
pathInstructions.windows.step3: Copy the path, then paste here.
pathInstructions.windows.tip: Alternatively, hold Shift and right-click the folder, then select "Copy as path".
pathInstructions.linux.step1: Open a terminal and navigate to the directory with cd.
pathInstructions.linux.step2: Run pwd to print the full path.
pathInstructions.linux.step3: Copy the output and paste here.
pathInstructions.linux.tip: In most file managers, Ctrl+L reveals the full path in the address bar.
agentConfig.choosePath.title: Specify path manually
agentConfig.choosePath.description: Browser security blocks apps from reading full local paths via a file picker. Copy the absolute path and paste it into the input.
agentConfig.choosePath.macStep2: Hold Option and right-click the folder.
agentConfig.choosePath.macStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
agentConfig.choosePath.windowsStep1: Find the folder in File Explorer.
agentConfig.choosePath.windowsStep2: Hold Shift and right-click the folder.
agentConfig.choosePath.windowsStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
agentConfig.choosePath.terminalStep3: Copy the output and paste it into the path input.
agentConfig.help.name: Display name for this agent.
agentConfig.help.title: Job title shown in the org chart.
agentConfig.help.role: Organizational role. Determines position and capabilities.
agentConfig.help.reportsTo: The agent this one reports to in the org hierarchy.
agentConfig.help.capabilities: Describes what this agent can do. Shown in the org chart and used for task routing.
agentConfig.help.adapterType: How this agent runs: local CLI (Claude/Codex/OpenCode), OpenClaw Gateway, spawned process, or generic HTTP webhook.
agentConfig.help.cwd: Deprecated legacy working directory fallback for local adapters. Existing agents may still carry this value, but new configurations should use project workspaces instead.
agentConfig.help.promptTemplate: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep this small and dynamic. Use it for current-task framing, not large static instructions. Supports {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, {{ agent.role }} and other template variables.
agentConfig.help.model: Override the default model used by the adapter.
agentConfig.help.thinkingEffort: Control model reasoning depth. Supported values vary by adapter/model.
agentConfig.help.chrome: Enable Claude's Chrome integration by passing --chrome.
agentConfig.help.dangerouslySkipPermissi: Run unattended by auto-approving adapter permission prompts when supported.
agentConfig.help.dangerouslyBypassSandbo: Run Codex without sandbox restrictions. Required for filesystem/network access.
agentConfig.help.search: Enable Codex web search capability during runs.
agentConfig.help.workspaceStrategy: How Paperclip should realize an execution workspace for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, or use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
agentConfig.help.workspaceBaseRef: Base git ref used when creating a worktree branch. Leave blank to use the resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
agentConfig.help.workspaceBranchTemplate: Template for naming derived branches. Supports {{issue.identifier}}, {{issue.title}}, {{agent.name}}, {{project.id}}, {{workspace.repoRef}}, and {{slug}}.
agentConfig.help.worktreeParentDir: Directory where derived worktrees should be created. Absolute, ~-prefixed, and repo-relative paths are supported.
agentConfig.help.runtimeServicesJson: Optional workspace runtime service definitions. Use this for shared app servers, workers, or other long-lived companion processes attached to the workspace.
agentConfig.help.maxTurnsPerRun: Maximum number of agentic turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
agentConfig.help.command: The command to execute (e.g. node, python).
agentConfig.help.localCommand: Override the path to the CLI command you want the adapter to call (e.g. /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
agentConfig.help.extraArgs: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
agentConfig.help.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
agentConfig.help.bootstrapPrompt: Only sent when Paperclip starts a fresh session. Use this for stable setup guidance that should not be repeated on every heartbeat.
agentConfig.help.payloadTemplateJson: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payloads before Paperclip adds its standard wake and workspace fields.
agentConfig.help.webhookUrl: The URL that receives POST requests when the agent is invoked.
agentConfig.help.heartbeatInterval: Run this agent automatically on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
agentConfig.help.timeoutSec: Maximum seconds a run can take before being terminated. 0 means no timeout.
agentConfig.help.graceSec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing the process.
agentConfig.help.wakeOnDemand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignments, API calls, UI actions, or automated systems.
agentConfig.help.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
agentConfig.help.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum number of heartbeat runs that can execute simultaneously for this agent.
agentConfig.help.budgetMonthlyCents: Monthly spending limit in cents. 0 means no limit.
